description: This figure presents simulated evolutions of microstructure and dislocation density distribution on the YZ surface of low-alloy steel under transient deformation conditions using a 3D cellular automaton model. Panels (a) and (b) illustrate the effects of transiently increasing strain rate from 0.01 s⁻¹ to 0.1 s⁻¹ at 0.2 and 0.4 strain under 950 °C, respectively, while panel (c) shows decreasing temperature from 1050 °C to 950 °C during the strain range of 0.4–0.5 at 0.01 s⁻¹. Each row contains paired microstructure maps—where white, green, and red grains represent the matrix, first-stage DRX, and second-stage DRX—and corresponding dislocation density maps with respective color scales. Quantified DRX fractions indicate that second-stage DRX grains progressively nucleate and consume earlier grains. As stated in the accompanying text, the lower dislocation density in DRX_GSII compared to both the matrix and DRX_GSI provides the driving force for grain refinement and stress softening during multi-stage recrystallization.
